description.en: A key short-duration pulse in Sri Lanka, cultivated on about 11,899 ha with annual production of ~12,544 t. DOA has released high-yielding varieties MI 5, MI 6, MI 7, Harsha, and Ari that dominate current adoption. Important in rice–legume crop rotations in the Dry and Intermediate zones; also grown in the sub-humid rain-fed uplands. Drought is the major yield-limiting factor.

duration_days.quote: Most varieties mature in 60 to 75 days

expected_yield_kg_per_acre.quote: Averages of 1000 kg/ha have been reported in Sri Lanka, though current yields around 800 kg ha⁻¹
expected_yield_kg_per_acre.review_notes: 800–1200 kg/ha typical SL range → 324–486 kg/acre. Potential is higher (3000 kg/ha) under best management.

description.quote: High-yielding varieties such as MI-5, MI-6, Ari, and MI-7 have increased farmer adoption in Sri Lanka. mung bean is a key short-duration pulse crop, cultivated on 11,899 hectares with an annual production of 12,544 tons.
